facts.text: Fashion models present clothing, accessories, and beauty products in runway shows, editorial shoots, and advertising campaigns.
Modern runway modeling grew out of Paris fashion houses in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
Charles Frederick Worth is often credited with helping pioneer the use of live models to present couture designs.
Editorial models appear in magazines and brand campaigns, while runway models specialize in walking fashion shows.
The term 'supermodel' became especially popular in the 1980s and 1990s.
Naomi Campbell, Cindy Crawford, Linda Evangelista, Christy Turlington, and Claudia Schiffer helped define the supermodel era.
Naomi Campbell became one of the first Black models to appear on the cover of French Vogue, marking a major moment in fashion history.
Tyra Banks helped break barriers for Black models in mainstream fashion magazines and major lingerie campaigns.
Kate Moss became a defining face of 1990s fashion and helped popularize a very different model aesthetic from the glam supermodel look.
Runway models often change outfits in just minutes backstage during major fashion week shows.
The biggest global fashion weeks are held in New York, London, Milan, and Paris.
Model agencies such as Ford, Elite, IMG, and Wilhelmina have played major roles in developing fashion careers.
A 'go-see' is an appointment where a model meets designers, photographers, or casting directors to be considered for work.
Comp cards, also called composite cards or zed cards, serve as a model's quick visual résumé.
Editorial photography often values mood, storytelling, and styling as much as the clothing itself.
The modeling world includes niches beyond fashion, including commercial, fitness, beauty, hand, plus-size, and lifestyle modeling.
Twiggy became an international sensation in the 1960s and is one of the most famous models of the decade.
Gisele Bündchen became one of the most successful models in the world and helped usher in a new era of high-earning global supermodels.
Some models become major business founders, launching skincare, fashion, beverage, or media brands after their runway careers.
Backstage fashion shows involve stylists, makeup artists, hair teams, dressers, and producers working in highly choreographed timing.
The walk, posture, and pace expected of a runway model can vary dramatically from one designer to another.
Fashion campaigns are often shot months before a season's clothes actually reach stores.
In recent years the industry has pushed more visibility for size diversity, age diversity, and broader representation across backgrounds.
Cover bookings for Vogue, Harper's Bazaar, and Elle have long been considered prestige milestones in a model's career.
The rise of social media allowed many models to build personal brands directly with fans instead of relying only on magazines and agencies.
Some of the most famous fashion photographs ever made were created by artists like Richard Avedon, Peter Lindbergh, Steven Meisel, and Irving Penn.
Model castings can involve hundreds of people competing for only a handful of looks in a single show.
Victoria's Secret runway shows once turned many models into household names around the world.
Beverly Johnson became the first Black model to appear on the cover of American Vogue in 1974, a landmark moment in fashion history.
Linda Evangelista became famous for her ability to completely reinvent her look from one shoot or runway season to the next.
Cindy Crawford's signature beauty mark became one of the most recognizable features of the 1990s supermodel era.
Claudia Schiffer was discovered in a nightclub in Dusseldorf and went on to become one of the defining faces of global fashion.
The phrase 'heroin chic' was used in the 1990s to describe a controversial fashion aesthetic associated with very thin, pale, waif-like looks.
Many people credit Gisele Bundchen with helping usher fashion away from the heroin-chic era and back toward a more athletic glamour.
Fashion Month traditionally moves in order from New York to London to Milan to Paris.
Haute couture garments are made to order, which is why couture runway fittings are extremely precise and time-intensive.
A fit model helps designers test how garments drape and move before a collection is finalized.
Simple unretouched 'digitals' or 'polaroids' are often used by agencies and clients to see what a model looks like without styling.
Bookers at modeling agencies match talent to castings, negotiate work, and help shape a model's schedule and image.
Commercial modeling often focuses on catalogs, e-commerce, and advertising rather than high-fashion editorials or runway.
Street casting is when brands or photographers discover and book models outside the traditional agency pipeline.
The 1990 George Michael video for 'Freedom! '90' helped cement the star power of the supermodel era by featuring several of the biggest runway names of the time.
Tyra Banks successfully crossed over from runway and lingerie campaigns into television, production, and entrepreneurship.
Heidi Klum turned modeling fame into a major television and business career, especially through Project Runway and her own brands.
Cara Delevingne became known for moving fluidly between fashion modeling, acting, and entertainment.
Runway shoes are often sample-sized and can be challenging to walk in, especially during fast backstage changes.
Many editorial shoots tell a visual story, which means a model is often performing a mood or character rather than simply showing clothes.
The best-known models of one era often influence beauty standards, brand marketing, and even how photographers compose an image.
Plus-size and curve modeling became more visible in the 2010s as the industry slowly broadened ideas of representation and beauty.
Some of the most in-demand models are known less for height or measurements than for a distinct face, memorable walk, and ability to project attitude.
Backstage at a major fashion show can feel like a carefully timed sprint, with hair, makeup, dressing, and lineup all happening at once.
A single runway appearance in a major closing or opening slot can dramatically raise a model's profile within the industry.
Fashion photography often becomes historic not just because of the clothes, but because of the model-photographer collaboration that defines an era.
Many models now balance agency work with personal content creation, making branding and audience-building part of the modern career.
Shalom Harlow became one of the most recognizable editorial and runway faces of the 1990s.
Christy Turlington was especially associated with grace, elegance, and long-term brand relationships in fashion campaigns.
Linda Evangelista famously said she did not get out of bed for less than ten thousand dollars a day, a quote that came to symbolize the supermodel era.
Iman became an international fashion icon and later built a highly successful beauty brand focused on underserved skin tones.
Adut Akech became one of the breakout runway stars of the late 2010s and early 2020s.
Ashley Graham became one of the best-known models associated with broader visibility for curve and plus-size representation.
A runway show's opening model often sets the tone for the entire collection, while the closing model is usually saved for a dramatic finish.
Editorial shoots often require hours of lighting, styling, and direction for just a few final published images.
Portfolio books and online agency pages help clients quickly understand a model's range, look, and experience.
E-commerce modeling has become a huge part of the business because online shopping relies on clear, consistent clothing presentation.
Many models study posing intensely because small shifts in angle, hands, and expression can completely change a photo.
A memorable runway walk is often described as having rhythm, presence, balance, and control rather than just speed.
Twiggy became one of the defining modeling faces of the 1960s.
Her look helped shape an entire era of fashion imagery.
Twiggy is one of the standout topics people mention when discussing Fashion Models.
